name: CenterMass
description: CenterMass is a Human spacecraft weapons and armor retailer with franchise stores in multiple locations across the United Empire of Earth (UEE). Founded as a weapons-only store, the company added armor to its inventory in 2939. It also keeps mechanics on staff for the installation, modification, and repair of any items purchased at a CenterMass store. == Locations == 2nd floor of the IO-North tower, Area18, ArcCorp The Plaza, New Babbage, microTech == Gallery == == References ==

name: Cubby Blast
description: Cubby Blast is a weapon dealership in Zone 1 of Area18 on ArcCorp. The store is owned and operated by the former outlaw Clor Vee, and it carries a catalog of personal weaponry, personal armor, and other defensive goods. == History == The Cubby Blast holo-jingle promises "racks of rifles, stacks of shotguns, piles of pistols," and the store has established itself as one of Area18's more eccentric locales. While most of the stores on the planet aim for an aura of respectability, Cubby Blast stands apart from the corporate culture that surrounds it. It is modeled after a weapons shop on the Spider station in the Cathcart system, and the name itself comes from a Cathcart sales phrase meaning "the weapon fires fine." This character has made Cubby Blast a popular destination for people traveling through the system, whether or not they intend to buy a weapon. The store is centered on its owner

name: Live Fire Weapons
description: Live Fire Weapons is a retail franchise that sells personal weapons and weapon attachments. Its stores operate from shopping galleries aboard space stations and orbital rest stops across the Stanton system. == Store locations == == References ==

name: Tammany and Sons
description: Tammany and Sons is a shop in Lorville owned by Hurston Dynamics. It primarily sells goods to the company's workers in a company-store arrangement that is intended to keep credits, and employees, within the Hurston ecosystem. == Location == The shop is located in the L19 Workers District of Lorville, Hurston, next to the miners' rail line. In addition to clothing and armor, it stocks Greycat Industrial's Pyro RYT Multi-Tool. It is also a source for the Gyson Ready-Up, a soft-body breathing helmet nicknamed the "baghead" that residents commonly use to cope with the planet's poor atmospheric quality. == Merchandise == == Gallery == == References ==

name: Casaba Outlet
description: Casaba Outlet is a human clothing retail franchise founded by the siblings Jean and Joel Polanco. It sells designer-style fashion at affordable prices and has grown from a single storefront on Lyre to a chain reaching from the Corel to Chronos systems across nearly the entire UEE. == History == Jean and Joel Polanco grew up aboard their family's Freelancer as their parents worked as independent cargo haulers, crisscrossing the systems in search of work. Jean became fascinated with the cultural differences between planets and began buying distinctive items on one world to resell on the next, realizing that a cheap trinket from one system could be a statement piece in another. The pair later shifted from selling single curiosities to buying remnant stock in bulk from small and medium softline manufacturers, and their ship became known in various landing zones for stylish yet affordable clo

name: Mining Support Center
description: The Mining Support Center is a location commonly found on Refinery Decks on Rest Stop stations. It contains a supply shop that sells various mining supplies. == References ==
